text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. First, time management helps you break large projects into smaller tasks, so you can track your progress and avoid last-minute panic. Prioritization is also essential—identify the most important or urgent activities and tackle them first. Additionally, scope management means you should clearly understand what is required and resist adding extra work without permission. Finally, communication with your team or manager is key; if you foresee a delay, let others know early so they can adjust their expectations. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less stress and greater success.
